class.name: ResonanceRuntime
class.brief_description: Scene hub for Nexus Resonance: initializes ResonanceServer from ResonanceRuntimeConfig, drives simulation, buses, probe notification, debug overlays, and optional FMOD bridge.
class.description: [b]Role:[/b] One [code]ResonanceRuntime[/code] per scene (or per root that should own Steam Audio) coordinates engine startup, per-frame simulation, Godot bus routing for direct/pathing/reverb, and editor/runtime debug overlays. It reads simulation and audio settings from [member runtime] ([url=#ResonanceRuntimeConfig]ResonanceRuntimeConfig[/url]) and passes them to [method ResonanceServer.init_audio_engine] via [method get_config_dict]. [b]Adding the node:[/b] Use Add Child Node and choose [b]ResonanceRuntime[/b] (global GDScript class [code]class_name ResonanceRuntime[/code] on [code]resonance_runtime.gd[/code], extends [code]Node[/code]; not a GDExtension type). If [member runtime] is empty, a default config resource is created automatically at runtime. [b]Listener and probes:[/b] Without a [url=#ResonanceListener]ResonanceListener[/url], the runtime updates the Steam Audio listener from the active camera. When [member runtime] reflection or pathing options change, it notifies [url=#ResonanceProbeVolume]ResonanceProbeVolume[/url] nodes so probe batches and visualization stay consistent. [b]Resource documentation:[/b] Sub-resources do not get an inspector documentation button. Open [url=#ResonanceRuntimeConfig]ResonanceRuntimeConfig[/url] from this page for HRTF, ray tracer, occlusion, reflections, pathing, simulation throttling, and bus names. [b]FMOD bridge:[/b] [member fmod_bridge_enabled] turns on integration between Steam Audio and FMOD Studio: the native [url=#ResonanceFMODBridge]ResonanceFMODBridge[/url] loads the Steam Audio [code]phonon_fmod[/code] plugin and shares context with FMOD. You need [url=https://github.com/utopia-rise/fmod-gdextension]fmod-gdextension[/url], a correct FMOD Studio setup, and the plugin files on the FMOD plugin path. See [code]docs/FMOD_BRIDGE.md[/code] in the repository for a step-by-step guide. [b]Debug overlays:[/b] When [member enable_debug] is [code]true[/code], [member debug_overlay_toggle_key] (default F1) toggles the status overlay, [member performance_overlay_toggle_key] (F2) the frame-time overlay, and [member player_overlay_toggle_key] (F3) source/occlusion and reflection ray visualization (subject to server debug support).

method.name: get_config_dict
method.description: Dictionary for [method ResonanceServer.init_audio_engine]: merges [url=#ResonanceRuntimeConfig]ResonanceRuntimeConfig[/url] data from [member runtime] with runtime-only flags such as [code]debug_occlusion[/code] (player ray overlay).

members.member: [url=#ResonanceRuntimeConfig]ResonanceRuntimeConfig[/url] resource. Create or link an existing [code].tres[/code]. Auto-created default if empty. All exported fields are documented on the linked class page.

When [code]true[/code], initializes the FMOD bridge ([url=#ResonanceFMODBridge]ResonanceFMODBridge[/url]) so FMOD events can use the shared Steam Audio simulation. Requires [url=https://github.com/utopia-rise/fmod-gdextension]fmod-gdextension[/url] and the Steam Audio [code]phonon_fmod[/code] plugin on the FMOD plugin path. See [code]docs/FMOD_BRIDGE.md[/code].
When false, debug/performance/player overlay toggle keys do nothing (for release builds). When turned off at runtime, open overlays and player ray viz are closed.
Key to toggle debug overlay at runtime (default: F1).
Key to toggle performance overlay at runtime (default: F2). Hidden until toggled on.
Key to toggle player/source ray visualization (occlusion + reflection rays; default: F3). Only processed when enable_debug is true.
